relationship: Independent confirmatory re-run of 2026-06-22-juristcu.json — same dataset, models and parameters, on a freshly seeded pgvector container. Recorded to show the headline numbers reproduce; the first file stays the canonical run.

stack.note: Mode verified live via GET /health -> {embedder: AzureOpenAI, vectorStore: pgvector} before measuring; not the keyless fakes.

retrieval.note: Only the semantic (Azure) path was re-measured this round; the lexical hash baseline was not re-run (see 2026-06-22-juristcu.json for it).

llm_judge.method: Answers generated once; the judge runs 5x over the fixed answers, so the standard deviation isolates judge variance rather than generator variance.

relevance.verdict: within margin (judge variance; absolute scores are indicative, per ADR 0011)

comparison_to_first_run.divergence_direction: one-directional in both runs — the lexical gate marks answers not-faithful that the judge scores groundedness 5/5; never the reverse. 2 of the 4 divergent queries are identical across runs.

provenance: Local run against Azure OpenAI on a fresh pgvector container. NOT produced by CI — ci.yml runs the keyless Layer 1 only (the judge skips without a key, by design). Reproduce with POST /eval/retrieval and POST /eval/judge (Eval__JudgeRuns=5) when a key is configured.
interpretation: Reproduces the headline pattern from 2026-06-22-juristcu.json: semantic recall@10 holds at ~42% and Layer 1 / Layer 2 agreement at 84% with the same one-directional divergence (the deterministic gate is conservative, not wrong). Hit-rate, MRR and relevance differ by a hair, within the variance the ADR already flags as indicative.
